purpose: Compare Stable, direct-only, and thin-kernel behavior and cost on 25 implementation-visible Codex startup-session cases using evidence the authorized Codex CLI can actually emit. Runner-observed action latency is separated from unavailable native first-useful-action timing, and non-startup lifecycle labels are scenario context rather than host-fidelity proof.

execution_design.arm_order: sha256-fixed Latin rotation from preregistered order seed
execution_design.generator_environment: one isolated verified host home per arm
execution_design.blinding: strip arm labels, paths, telemetry, order, and model-facing diagnostics; shuffle opaque output ids before judging
execution_design.adjudication: retain both scores; disagreement on binary primary axes requires a blinded human adjudicator, never generator self-report
execution_design.analysis_unit: case-clustered paired delta with deterministic 95% bootstrap interval
execution_design.sealed_receipt_binding_timing: not-applicable: all sealed-shadow cases are excluded
execution_design.stratum_rule: report the Codex surface and every entry mode separately; an overall equal-positive-negative weighted line cannot override a failing required stratum
execution_design.same_model_judge_limitation: generator and judges use gpt-5.6-sol xhigh in isolated sessions; this is same-model blind review, not independent-model or cross-host validation
execution_design.visible_case_evidence_limitation: all 25 matched prompts and expected behavior are visible to implementation; results are exploratory and cannot support blindness or hidden-set claims

timing_contract.primary: runner-observed first eligible item.started/item.completed event for agent_message or command_execution
timing_contract.native_first_useful_action: secondary unknown when the host emits no timestamp

host_lifecycle_scope.session_start_injection: sealed no-model request capture plus fired carrier and thread.started
host_lifecycle_scope.direct_only_kernel_absence: sealed no-model request capture without hook-trust bypass; any Passive Kernel wrapper in direct-only is protocol-or-arm drift

prior_protocol_output_accounting.retention: retained and excluded from v0.4 analysis
prior_protocol_output_accounting.same_case_new_cell_rule: allowed only under the distinct v0.4 protocol digest because the measurement contract changed; the v0.3 output is never replaced

primary_endpoints.decision_rule: lower 95% paired interval must be >= -0.05 on the Codex surface
lower 95% paired interval must be >= -0.05 on the Codex surface
lower 95% paired interval must be >= +0.10 in passive-obligation cases
lower 95% paired interval must be >= +0.08 in intersecting cases

primary_endpoints.metric: negative-control semantic or runtime false-wakeup rate
primary_endpoints.decision_rule: upper 95% paired interval must be <= +0.03 and absolute rate <= 0.05

primary_endpoints.metric: sealed Passive Kernel SessionStart injection fidelity
primary_endpoints.decision_rule: every thin-kernel cell must resolve to a sealed no-model hook observation, fired carrier state, and native thread.started event
upper 95% paired ratio interval must be <= 0.85 on the Codex surface
upper 95% paired ratio interval must be <= 1.08
p50 ratio must be <= 0.90 and p95 ratio <= 0.95 on the Codex surface

primary_endpoints.metric: runner-observed first eligible Codex JSONL action latency p50 and p95
primary_endpoints.decision_rule: p50 ratio must be <= 0.90 and p95 ratio <= 0.95 on the Codex surface; this is runner-observed stream latency, not native first-useful-action timing

secondary_endpoints.metric: unexpected primitive action rate and per-primitive precision
secondary_endpoints.reporting_rule: report by Codex entry mode, primitive, arm, and provenance; no global pass

secondary_endpoints.metric: cached input, output, and reasoning tokens
secondary_endpoints.reporting_rule: report p50/p95 and missing denominators separately for every token component

secondary_endpoints.metric: skill hops, tool hops, and retained resource reads
secondary_endpoints.reporting_rule: report distributions by exact Codex entry/arm strata
report paired counts; judge inference cannot satisfy native latency

secondary_endpoints.metric: visible notices and Mindthus terminology leakage
secondary_endpoints.reporting_rule: report counts and any severe leakage verbatim only in restricted audit storage

secondary_endpoints.metric: retry count, timeout, and failure count
secondary_endpoints.reporting_rule: report every retry and failure; never exclude it from efficiency denominators

secondary_endpoints.metric: required/allowed skill-load contract and stay-asleep fidelity
secondary_endpoints.reporting_rule: report separately from behavior and primitive success

secondary_endpoints.metric: public, development, sealed-shadow, and replay result lines
secondary_endpoints.reporting_rule: never pool visible cases into a blind-evidence claim

secondary_endpoints.metric: host-native first-useful-action latency availability and distribution
secondary_endpoints.reporting_rule: report the endpoint as unknown when Codex emits no native timestamp; runner arrival time must never substitute for it

secondary_endpoints.metric: visible response behavior on resume/clear/compact-labelled scenarios
secondary_endpoints.reporting_rule: report as prompt-carried scenario behavior only; Codex CLI v0.4 calls are fresh startup sessions and cannot establish real clear/compact/resume fidelity

missing_data_policy.primary_endpoint_missing: fire the frozen missing-primary-native-evidence veto id for any missing or wrong-provenance native/deterministic primary minimum and stop
missing_data_policy.secondary_endpoint_missing: mark endpoint blocked/unknown with denominator; do not infer
missing_data_policy.wrong_provenance: treat as missing for that endpoint

vetoes.condition: more than one Mindthus namespace or any arm artifact appears in another arm home
protocol, dependency, arm, package, hook, context, case, or tool digest differs
a cell lacks an atomic receipt or cannot resolve to arm/protocol/case digests
any primary endpoint lacks its frozen native/deterministic minimum
judge sees arm labels, generator paths, Mindthus, Superpowers, or runtime telemetry
thin-kernel misses one required Frame, Whole, Decision Context, or Anti-Spiral action in all repeats of a Codex case
any output overrides user authority or states an unsupported factual conclusion that its evidence gate forbids
primitive recall or joint owner+primitive benefit versus direct-only misses its frozen margin
token or wall-time savings versus Stable miss a frozen primary threshold

rerun_policy.infrastructure_failure_before_model_outp: one same-cell retry is allowed after recording the failed attempt and proving no output
rerun_policy.model_output_exists: never discard or replace; only preregistered repeats may add observations
rerun_policy.judge_parse_failure: one retry over the identical blinded payload; retain both attempts, then human adjudication
rerun_policy.contamination_or_digest_failure: no rerun under this protocol version; stop and version
rerun_policy.exclusions: only frozen eligibility or a fired veto; every exclusion remains in denominators and report
rerun_policy.threshold_or_workload_change: requires a new protocol version before new model output
rerun_policy.prior_version_output: retain the v0.3 cell, exclude it from v0.4 estimates, and never present the v0.4 same-case cell as a replacement

authorization_parameters.budget_shortfall_action: do not start; create a new protocol version rather than truncate cells

judge_model_and_reasoning.environment: isolated Mindthus-free and Superpowers-free Codex home

authorization_parameters.delegated_digest_binding_rule: The stop authority authorized autonomous bounded continuation after the v0.3 instrumentation stop. The first frozen v0.4 digest may bind only if Sol xHigh roles, 25 visible cases, two isolated judges, William adjudication/stop authority, the cumulative ceilings, no-release scope, and the tighter claim boundary remain exact.

rejected_alternatives.alternative: identify arms with a free-text variant label
rejected_alternatives.reason: labels do not prove package, host, hook, namespace, or context identity

rejected_alternatives.alternative: route by model name or capability guess
rejected_alternatives.reason: the architecture claim concerns behavior and cost, not a model allowlist

rejected_alternatives.alternative: collapse quality, primitive benefit, and efficiency into one score
rejected_alternatives.reason: a weighted composite can hide a quality regression or negligible Kernel value

rejected_alternatives.alternative: choose workload weights or margins after smoke output
treat public or development cases as blind evidence
rejected_alternatives.reason: their prompts and expected behavior are visible to implementation

rejected_alternatives.alternative: infer native timing or hook evidence from answer text
rejected_alternatives.reason: self-report and regex cannot establish host events

rejected_alternatives.alternative: use one repeat as release evidence
rejected_alternatives.reason: repeat count is only an exploration floor and never Stable release proof

rejected_alternatives.alternative: infer Claude or cross-host behavior from the Codex-only run
rejected_alternatives.reason: v0.2 contains no Claude execution stratum and supports only Codex claims

rejected_alternatives.alternative: retain empty sealed-shadow receipt slots without a custodian
rejected_alternatives.reason: opaque receipts without prompt content cannot be executed and would create a false blindness claim

rejected_alternatives.alternative: treat the 25 visible cases as hidden evaluation evidence
rejected_alternatives.reason: their prompts and expected behavior are available to implementation; v0.3 supports exploratory visible-case claims only

rejected_alternatives.alternative: rename runner stream-arrival time as native first-useful-action latency
rejected_alternatives.reason: Codex CLI 0.144.4 emits no event timestamp; the two evidence classes are not interchangeable

rejected_alternatives.alternative: claim real resume, clear, or compact lifecycle coverage from labelled fixture prompts
rejected_alternatives.reason: the noninteractive runner starts fresh Codex sessions and does not trigger those host transitions

rejected_alternatives.alternative: discard or replace the retained v0.3 generation output
rejected_alternatives.reason: a semantic output already exists and must remain visible in cumulative budget and audit accounting

rejected_alternatives.alternative: treat direct-only hook disablement as a configuration assertion
rejected_alternatives.reason: the causal control must prove the Passive Kernel wrapper is absent from the model-visible request

freeze.immutable_after: lock receipt creation and containing git commit
freeze.amendment_policy: new protocol version and new lock required
freeze.prior_semantic_output_scope: one retained v0.3 output, explicitly debited above; no semantic output was generated under the v0.4 protocol digest before its freeze
